item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh goodness, this is great. Should have read this sooner.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article has a very nice sense of escalation which easily draws me in every time I decide to read it. I particularly get a lot of enjoyment from reading the new, strange adaptations the bodies are progressively obtaining to get out of the grave. Very subtle, creepy stuff. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the autopsy of 2679-B-129
The following is written

Pelvic bone used to determine sex (female).

Implying subject is female
However when the examiner tests for the chromosomes, there two XX's and a Y implying a male with Klinefelter syndrome, why is the subject male if the pelvic bone suggests otherwise. Doesn't the Y chromosome carry the testes determining factor?
Maybe I'm just dumb but this confused me.

Otherwise a good read
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most likely reason is that grave appears only when that thing (it certainly is no longer human anymore) dies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get why they haven't tried digging up a fresh one?

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

This is a sad skip. Very sad. One potential crosslink could be to SCP-3422 which involves another instance of a corpse that produces animate clones of itself. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2679-A instances are graves found throughout SCP-2679. As many as 3 new instances of SCP-2679-A have been observed to emerge in a single day. Tombstones will extend upward from the soil (a process which takes 4 to 12 hours). These tombstones vary in size and shape, but are otherwise non-anomalous. Each bears the name 'JEANNETTE PARSLOV'. Coffins appear approximately 1 to 4 meters beneath each instance, and contain an instance of SCP-2679-B. Instances of SCP-2679-B are the remains of various organisms.

To this:

SCP-2679-A instances are graves found throughout SCP-2679. As many as 3 new instances of SCP-2679-A have been observed to emerge in a single day. Tombstones will extend upward from the soil (a process which takes 4 to 12 hours). These tombstones vary in size and shape, but are otherwise non-anomalous. Each bears the name 'JEANNETTE PARSLOV'. Coffins appear approximately 1 to 4 meters beneath each instance, and contain an instance of SCP-2679-B. Instances of SCP-2679-B are the remains of various organisms.1

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Similarities between the effects of SCP-2679 and SCP-3422 have been noted. Investigation into a possible connection between the two phenomena is ongoing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did iteration 075 claw out her eyes?

Also am I the only person who thinks there could be an interesting Tale regarding the distant descendants of the Parslov family in the Bellerverse and their protector? A horrifying monstrosity, no longer even vaguely humanoid which can't be permanently killed since it just respawns with new adaptations so the same method of demise won't work twice and it still recognizes, loves and defends the Parslovs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: https://i.redd.it/luwdamlep8yz.jpg

item.title: Re: Minor Medical terms possible Upgrades
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback! (And because I'm too tired to go through each comment, I also want to say thanks to everyone who gave feedback)

I plan on revisiting this at some point in the near future to make a few quick/useful tweaks; when I do, I'll comb through this thread again to make sure I'm fixing all the nits that need fixing — this one ('acute albinism') will definitely be among them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Horror autopsy" is one of my favourite sub-genres, really happy to see it done so well here.

I've enjoyed your recent submissions for being genuinely creepy, and for exercising so much restraint. I feel like a lot of submissions try to fully explain every single thing, but IMO less is always more with this sort of thing.

item.title: Minor Medical terms possible Upgrades
item.content:encoded:
Hi there! Liked the tone of neutral necropsies describing the horror. Probably no one else got annoyed by this but for sake of perfection:

acute is a word describing a symptom or sign time-wise ("sudden onset"). I suspect that's not what you wanted for eg acute albinism. That would be severe, or total, or marked, or complete, whatever you prefer to signal extension as opposed to duration.
extruded is fine, but enucleated is the same and sounds creepier.

That said, as an MD i thoroughly enjoyed the autopsies, perfect tone, great immersion.

Someone said below that analysis could be extended to coffin and stone. Made me wonder if as this creature strays further away from humankind, the coffin could stop being a problem and slowly turn in… an egg?

Also: why foundation hasn't tried to locate the Parslovs? That gravestone sounds like really effective thaumaturgic technology, almost Able-esque!

A lot of possible ramifications, sign of a healthy, thriving SCP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Redactions imply that the current level of clearance don't get that information, but a higher/specialized level does. Presumably, this is at the highest relevant clearance.

I agree on the note about the family, there's a clear connection there that the Foundation would probably do something about.

item.title: Re: Can we dig up a live one?
item.content:encoded: I agree with this comment. Why haven't they dug one up before she dies is the first thought I had while reading the article. It really needs to explain either why they haven't tried or why they haven't succeeded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As many as 3 new instances of SCP-2679-A have been observed to emerge in a single day.

It would be ~1000 a year.

It might as well be Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I read it, it meant that the sex is indeterminate from external examination. They have to open up the body to get an idea of what sex it is most like.

Side note, I really feel sorry for the poor girl, she has to die again and again to try to get out of being buried alive, still can't get out even after sacrificing most of her physical humanity to have a better chance at it. One wonders why the Foundation didn't try harder to analyze the timing between appearances, or set up seismic sensors to dig up an iteration that hasn't died yet - I imagine it would be much better for the world if they got one out earlier on that was mostly human, rather than waiting for when she turns herself into a total monstrosity and succeeds by herself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm gonna be that guy, but wouldn't the Foundation redact the name and exact location of the graveyard, at least in part? Considering the object isn't categorized as safe and isn't (fully) in Foundation containment, that seems like standard policy.

Also, you might want to consider adding a small addendum regarding the Foundation's research regarding Jeannette's family, even if none have been found. Considering the self-replicating nature of the object, I feel like this is a step they would have taken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The second to last autopsy report says the sex is indeterminate, then later says female. Was that a copy paste error from the last one perhaps?

Also, I do wonder as cosmic above why they don't try to dig up a live one, but my guess is they manifest already post death/struggle.

It doesn't seem, though, like the graveyard will stop spreading once it claws its way to the surface… It needs to make it back to its family. If they exist. And if it can't survive on the surface in its current state, time for more iterations. And of the foundation stops it and locks it up… Time for iterations better at escaping the foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the creepier things I've read since the 3000 contest.

item.title: Can we dig up a live one?
item.content:encoded:
Hi, I think this is a very creative story and it has the right creepy factor where every iteration comes back more monstrous and less human. That reminds me a bit of the Xenomorph from Alien.

I have a question though. If we know there could be 3 new instances of graves per day, and the minimum depth is only 1 meter, why haven't we tried to dig up a fresh one? Someone should monitor the site and the moment a new one appears, get a huge Caterpillar backhoe and dig that up. Chances are good it may not have suffocated yet and a live specimen may be obtained. This could potentially halt the expansion of the cemetery, once it has successfully "come back."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea of the ever-expanding graveyard, and the bodies slowly becoming less human and more adept at surviving underground (albinism was a nice hint by the way) was pretty cool.
One criticism though, while obviously a lot of time and research was put into the autopsy logs, the actual description of the scip seems a little lackluster, and a few details could've been added to make it seem more realistic (what kind of material are the tombstones made of? The coffins? Do they emerge from the ground whole or build up as they go?)

+1 anyways though, love the creativity

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I was just "Meh" throughout the piece. But

… oh god this strongly reminds of SCP-349, and in a very good way. Yeah, they're kind of really similar, especially about that "expanding cemetery" idea, but those X-ray images really add to the immersion. So, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed — thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh man, this encompasses nearly everything that I enjoy reading in a scip. The concept is unique, the writing flows smoothly, the logs are unnerving, and the final note adds a flavour of tragedy without detracting from the horror.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of the Prime Ministerial Pet Cemetery in some aspects, but aside from that the idea of a Swiss Army Corpse trying to desperately make its way to the surface for an unknown, sinister-sounding purpose very much appeals to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so as long as they keep up on exhuming them

The key phrase here. It requires regular maintenance of a rather inconvenient variety, and it won't stop.

On a secondary factor, the -B instances also seem to be slowly becoming less human and increasingly adept at trying to dig their way to the surface. What happens when they actually succeed is a question that the Foundation probably does not want to know the answer to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still seems a bit more on the Euclid side to me, the graves seems to only pop up in that one cemetery so as long as they keep up on exhuming them, it should remain contained. They don't have to stop them from spawning, just from spawning outside the containment area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just noticed: logs 129 and 526 reference a Judith Blair and a Julia Blair. Are these meant to be the same person?

Otherwise, pretty great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! As for it being Keter:

It self-replicates, it's expanding, the Foundation doesn't know how to stop it (the coffins just keep appearing in the ground), and — based on the file names of the autopsies — it's spawned over 900 grave-sites in two to three years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Original concept, pretty solid. However, why is it keter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure why that came to mind, honestly.

Probably because of the distinct theme of the SCP coming back wrong paired with an anomalous graveyard?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me a bit of the South Park episode where Butters fakes his death and his parents bury his "corpse" in an "Indian graveyard." When Butters comes home, perfectly healthy, his parents assume the graveyard worked but now their son is demonic.

Not sure why that came to mind, honestly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Work it harder
Make it better
Do it faster
Makes us stronger

SOURCES:

  • Images from here, here, here, and here.

FEEDBACK:

  • Pedantique - ([SCP-3442] - Innovative and Synergistic Customer-Directed Actualization)
  • RubySlax
